introSection: Tormenta Rampaging Run is a steel roller coaster built by Swiss firm Bolliger & Mabillard currently under construction at Six Flags Over Texas in Arlington, Texas, USA. On completion, it will be the tallest Dive Coaster installation and the first to be in the giga coaster height range (300-399 ft), as well as being the tallest roller coaster to have inversions and a beyond vertical drop.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The ride was first publicly acknowledged in a press release on November 14, 2024.

The first components arrived on site in May 2025. A piece of track was placed within the park alongside a sign that said "We're building excitement for 2026!". Concrete footers began to be poured by June.

The ride's construction went vertical by September 23.

The ride was announced on September 25, 2025.

By October, the brake run and station track pieces had been installed.

On November 18, 2025, two cars from the train were revealed to the public. One unveiling took place at the park and the other in Orlando, Florida, USA at Bolliger & Mabillard's booth at the 2025 IAAPA Expo.

The highest point of the ride was topped off on December 19, 2025.

The first inversion, the tallest immelmann inversion, was topped off in January 2026.

In early February the second inversion, the vertical loop, was completed.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Tormenta: Rampaging Run will have the tallest vertical loop in the world, at tall.

fullText:
Tormenta Rampaging Run is a steel roller coaster built by Swiss firm Bolliger & Mabillard currently under construction at Six Flags Over Texas in Arlington, Texas, USA. On completion, it will be the tallest Dive Coaster installation and the first to be in the giga coaster height range (300-399 ft), as well as being the tallest roller coaster to have inversions and a beyond vertical drop.

The ride was first publicly acknowledged in a press release on November 14, 2024.

The first components arrived on site in May 2025. A piece of track was placed within the park alongside a sign that said "We're building excitement for 2026!". Concrete footers began to be poured by June.

The ride's construction went vertical by September 23.

The ride was announced on September 25, 2025.

By October, the brake run and station track pieces had been installed.

On November 18, 2025, two cars from the train were revealed to the public. One unveiling took place at the park and the other in Orlando, Florida, USA at Bolliger & Mabillard's booth at the 2025 IAAPA Expo.

The highest point of the ride was topped off on December 19, 2025.

The first inversion, the tallest immelmann inversion, was topped off in January 2026.

In early February the second inversion, the vertical loop, was completed.

Tormenta: Rampaging Run will have the tallest vertical loop in the world, at tall.
